title: Relate number lines to fraction bars
description: Practice using fractions bars to show fractions on a number line.
topic_path: Relate number lines to fraction bars

assessment_items.question_content:
**What fraction is located at Point $\blueD{A}$ on the number line?**


[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{A} =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Each whole has $4$ equal parts, so each part has a length of $\dfrac{1}{4}$.



[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]

Point $A$ is located at $\dfrac{3}{4}$.

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, two unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark labeled Point A, a tick mark at 1, and two unlabeled tick marks. Four equal rectangles are above the number line between the tick mark at 0 and the tick mark at 1. Each rectangle represents one interval on the number line. Three rectangles from the left are shaded and the fourth rectangle is not shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which point is at $\dfrac{2}{​6}$ on the number line?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each whole has $6$ equal parts, so each part has a length of $\dfrac{1}{6}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

We need to shade $\dfrac26$.  



[[☃ image 1]]



What point is located at $\dfrac26$?
Point $B$ is located at $\dfrac{2}{6}$.

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, a tick mark labeled Point A, a tick mark at point B, a tick mark at point C, a tick mark at point D, an unlabeled tick mark, a tick mark at 1, and an unlabeled tick mark. Six equal rectangles are above the number line between the tick mark at 0 and the tick mark at 1. Each rectangle represents one interval on the number line.
